questions.question: Where is this verse found? '¶ Go to the ant, thou sluggard; consider her ways, and be wise:'
questions.explanation: The verse '¶ Go to the ant, thou sluggard; consider her ways, and be wise:' is found in Proverbs 6:6-11.

questions.question:
Fill in the blanks for Proverbs 6:6-11:
¶ Go to the ant, _____ _____; consider her ways, and be _____:
questions.explanation: The full text is: ¶ Go to the ant, thou sluggard; consider her ways, and be wise:

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'But above all things, my brethren, swear not, neither by heaven, neither by the earth, neither by any other oath: but let your yea be yea; and your nay, nay; lest ye fall into condemnation.'
questions.explanation: The verse 'But above all things, my brethren, swear not, neither by heaven, neither by the earth, neither by any other oath: but let your yea be yea; and your nay, nay; lest ye fall into condemnation.' is found in James 5:12.

questions.question:
Fill in the blanks for James 5:12:
But above all things, my brethren, swear not, neither by heaven, _____ by the _____, neither by any other oath: but let your yea be yea; and your nay, nay; lest ye _____ into condemnation.
questions.explanation: The full text is: But above all things, my brethren, swear not, neither by heaven, neither by the earth, neither by any other oath: but let your yea be yea; and your nay, nay; lest ye fall into condemnation.

questions.explanation: The full text is: To every thing there is a season, and a time to every purpose under the heaven:

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Thou wilt surely wear away, both thou, and this people that is with thee: for this thing is too heavy for thee; thou art not able to perform it thyself alone.18.18 Thou wilt…: Heb. Fading thou wilt fade'
